name: trelby

metadata.name: trelby
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)

documentation:
Trelby is a simple, powerful, full-featured, multi-platform program for
writing movie screenplays. It is simple, fast and elegantly laid out to
make screenwriting simple, and it is infinitely configurable.

Features:

 * Screenplay editor: Enforces correct script format and pagination,
   auto-completion, and spell checking.
 * Multiplatform: Behaves identically on all platforms, generating the exact
   same output.
 * Choice of view: Multiple views, including draft view, WYSIWYG mode,
   and fullscreen to suit your writing style.
 * Name database: Character name database containing over 200,000 names
   from various countries.
 * Reporting: Scene/location/character/dialogue reports.
 * Compare: Ability to compare scripts, so you know what changed between
   versions.
 * Import: Screenplay formatted text, Final Draft XML (.fdx)
    and Celtx (.celtx).
 * Export: PDF, formatted text, HTML, RTF, Final Draft XML (.fdx).
 * PDF: Built-in, highly configurable PDF generator. Supports embedding your
   chosen font. Also supports generating PDFs with custom watermarks,
   to help track shared files.
 * Free software: Licensed under the GPL, Trelby welcomes developers and
   screenwriters to contribute in making it more useful.

functions.name: tests.test_locations.ch
documentation: test locations.Locations
helper test function.

functions.name: tests.test_join_elems.testForcedLb
documentation: tests element joining
we used to have a bug where if the latter element contained a forced
linebreak the result was invalid. this one tests the case where the
forced linebreak is on the first line of the element, the second one
where it is on the third line.

functions.name: tests.test_copypaste.testPasteAfterForcedLineBreak
documentation: tests delete commands
we had a bug where pasting on an empty line right after a forced
linebreak would think "oh, I'm in an empty element, I'll just change the
linetype of this line and be done with it", which is wrong, because the
linetypes of the lines above were not changed, and now you had an
element with multiple different linetypes. the right thing to do is not
change the linetypes at all unless the entire element is empty.

documentation: def testToUTF8():
u.init()
t = util.toUTF8
assert t("") == ""
assert t("yo") == "yo"
assert t("yö") == "yÃ¶"
def testFromUTF8():
u.init()
f = util.fromUTF8
assert f("") == ""
assert f("yo") == "yo"
assert f("yÃ¶") == "yö"
assert f("a\xE2\x82\xACb") == "ab"

methods.name: tests.test_myimport.TextImportMatcher.__eq__
documentation:
The text import has some known limitations:
    - depending on the export config, some lines are all caps, so it can't reliably preserve case
    - it can't reliably detect linebreak types
    - sometimes, it can't distinguish ACTION from SCENE types
That's why this implementation is not so hard on it, and only compares the text case-insensitively, doesn't
compare linebreak types at all and only compares the line type if it's not ACTION or SCENE

functions.name: tests.test_cut.testFirstDelete

functions.name: tests.test_cut.testTypeConvert
documentation: test that when joining two elements of different type, the latter of

is rewrapped correctly.

classes.name: trelby.mypager.Pager
documentation: used to iteratively add PML pages to a document

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.TextString
documentation: a piece of text on screen.

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.DisplayPage
documentation: a page shown on screen.

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.PageCache
documentation: caches pml.Pages for operations that repeatedly construct them over and
over again without the page contents changing.

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.getScreen
documentation: get a description of what the current screen contains. returns
(texts, dpages), where texts = [TextString, ...], dpages =
[DisplayPage, ...]. dpages is None if draft mode is in use or
doExtra is False. doExtra has same meaning as for generatePMLPage
otherwise. pageCache, if given, is used in layout mode to cache PML
pages. it should only be given when doExtra = False as the cached
pages aren't accurate down to that level.
partial lines (some of the the text is clipped off-screen) are only
included in the results if 'partials' is True.
lines in 'texts' have to be in monotonically increasing order, and
this has to always return at least one line.

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.getLineHeight
documentation: return height for one line on screen

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.getPageWidth
documentation: return width of one page in (floating point) pixels

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.drawTexts
documentation: see MyCtrl.OnPaint for what tl is. note: this is only a default
implementation, feel free to override this.

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.pos2linecol
documentation: determine what (line, col) is at position (x, y) (screen
coordinates) and return that, or (None, None) if (x, y) points
outside a page.

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.makeLineVisible
documentation: make line, which is not currently visible, visible. texts =
self.getScreen(ctrl, False)[0].

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.pageCmd
documentation: handle page up (dir == -1) or page down (dir == 1) command. cursor
is guaranteed to be visible when this is called, and auto-completion
to be off. cs = CommandState. texts and dpages are the usual.

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.pos2linecolGeneric
documentation: semi-generic implementation, for use by Draft and Layout modes.

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.makeLineVisibleGeneric
documentation: semi-generic implementation, for use by Draft and Layout modes.

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode._makeLineVisibleHelper
documentation: helper function for makeLineVisibleGeneric

methods.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewMode.pageCmdGeneric
documentation: semi-generic implementation, for use by Draft and Layout modes.
View Mode, i.e. a way of displaying the script on screen. this is an
abstract superclass.

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewModeDraft
documentation: Draft view mode. No fancy page break layouts, just text lines on a plain
background.

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewModeLayout
documentation: Layout view mode. Pages are shown with the actual layout they would
have.

classes.name: trelby.viewmode.ViewModeSideBySide
documentation: Side by side view mode. Pages are shown with the actual layout they
would have, as many pages at a time as fit on the screen, complete pages
only, in a single row.

modules.name: trelby.pml
documentation: PML is short for Page Modeling Language, our own neat little PDF-wannabe
format for expressing a script's complete contents in a neutral way
that's easy to render to almost anything, e.g. PDF, Postscript, Windows
GDI, etc.
A PML document is a collection of pages plus possibly some metadata.
Each page is a collection of simple drawing commands, executed
sequentially in the order given, assuming "complete overdraw" semantics
on the output device, i.e. whatever is drawn completely covers things it
is painted on top of.
All measurements in PML are in (floating point) millimeters.

functions.name: trelby.pml.genLine
documentation: helper function for creating simple lines

classes.name: trelby.pml.Document

methods.name: trelby.pml.Document.__init__
documentation: (w, h) is the size of each page.
A single document.

classes.name: trelby.pml.TOCItem
documentation: Table of content item (Outline item, in PDF lingo)

classes.name: trelby.pml.PDFFontInfo
documentation: information about one PDF font

classes.name: trelby.pml.DrawOp
documentation: An abstract base class for all drawing operations.

classes.name: trelby.pml.TextOp

methods.name: trelby.pml.TextOp.__init__
documentation:
---
param angle: one of 45, 90
param line: index of line in `Screenplay.lines`, or -1 if some other text. only used
  when drawing display, pdf output doesn't use this.
param size: the font size
---
Draw text string 'text', at position (x, y) mm from the upper left
corner of the page. Font used is 'size' points, and Courier / Times/
Helvetica as indicated by the flags, possibly being bold / italic /
underlined. angle is None, or an integer from 0 to 360 that gives the
slant of the text counter-clockwise from x-axis.

classes.name: trelby.pml.LineOp
documentation: Draw consecutive lines. 'points' is a list of (x, y) pairs (minimum 2
pairs) and 'width' is the line width, with 0 being the thinnest possible
line. if 'isClosed' is True, the last point on the list is connected to
the first one.

classes.name: trelby.pml.RectOp
documentation: Draw a rectangle, possibly filled, with specified lineWidth (which can
be -1 if fillType is FILL). (x, y) is position of upper left corner.

classes.name: trelby.pml.QuarterCircleOp
documentation: Draw a quarter circle centered at (x, y) with given radius and line
width. By default it will be the upper left quadrant of a circle, but
using the flip[XY] parameters you can choose other quadrants.

classes.name: trelby.pml.PDFOp

methods.name: trelby.pml.PDFOp.__init__
documentation:
---
param cmds: the straight PDF code to be inserted into the file
---
Arbitrary PDF commands. Should not have whitespace in the beginning or
the end. Should be used only for non-critical things like tweaking line
join styles etc, because non-PDF renderers will ignore these.

prefix lines other than the first one with.
create a PML document containing text (possibly linewrapped) divided
into pages automatically.

documentation: if w2 is closer to w1 in Levenshtein distance than d, add it to

modules.name: trelby.util
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: trelby.util.upper
documentation: like string.upper/lower/capitalize, but we do our own charset-handling
that doesn't need locales etc

functions.name: trelby.util.toLatin1
documentation: return 's', which must be a unicode string, converted to a ISO-8859-1
8-bit string. characters not representable in ISO-8859-1 are discarded.

functions.name: trelby.util.toUTF8
documentation: return 's', which must be a string of ISO-8859-1 characters, converted
to UTF-8.

functions.name: trelby.util.fromUTF8
documentation: return 's', which must be a string of UTF-8 characters, converted to
ISO-8859-1, with characters not representable in ISO-8859-1 discarded
and any invalid UTF-8 sequences ignored.

functions.name: trelby.util.isValidInputChar
documentation: returns True if kc (key-code) is a valid character to add to the script.

functions.name: trelby.util.toInputStr
documentation: return s with all non-valid input characters converted to valid input
characters, except form feeds, which are just deleted.

functions.name: trelby.util.removeFancyUnicode
documentation: replace fancy unicode characters with their ASCII/Latin1 equivalents.

functions.name: trelby.util.cleanInput
documentation: transform external input (unicode) into a form suitable for having in a
script

functions.name: trelby.util.replace
documentation: replace s[start:start + width] with toInputStr(new) and return s

functions.name: trelby.util.deleteChars
documentation: delete all characters in 'chars' from s and return that.

functions.name: trelby.util.fixNL
documentation: returns s with all possible different types of newlines converted to
unix newlines, i.e. a single "\n"

functions.name: trelby.util.clamp
documentation: clamps the given value to a specific range. both limits are optional.

functions.name: trelby.util.clampObj
documentation: like clamp, but gets/sets value directly from given object

functions.name: trelby.util.str2float
documentation: convert given string to float, clamping it to the given range
(optional). never throws any exceptions, return defVal (possibly clamped
as well) on any errors.

functions.name: trelby.util.str2int
documentation: like str2float, but for ints.

functions.name: trelby.util.listify
documentation: extract 'name' field from each item in 'seq', put it in a list, and
return that list.

functions.name: trelby.util.pct
documentation: return percentage of 'val1' of 'val2' (both ints) as an int (50% -> 50
etc.), or 0 if val2 is 0.

functions.name: trelby.util.pctf
documentation: return percentage of 'val1' of 'val2' (both ints/floats) as a float (50%
-> 50.0 etc.), or 0.0 if val2 is 0.0

functions.name: trelby.util.safeDiv
documentation: return float(val1) / val2, or 0.0 if val2 is 0.0

functions.name: trelby.util.safeDivInt
documentation: return float(val1) / val2, or 0.0 if val2 is 0

functions.name: trelby.util.flags2bools
documentation: for each character in 'flags', starting at beginning, checks if that
character is found in 's'. if so, appends True to a tuple, False
otherwise. returns that tuple, whose length is of course is len(flags).

functions.name: trelby.util.bools2flags
documentation: reverse of flags2bools. is given a number of objects, if each object
evaluates to true, chars[i] is appended to the return string. len(chars)
== len(bools) must be true.

functions.name: trelby.util.escapeStrings
documentation: return items, which is a list of ISO-8859-1 strings, as a single string
with \n between each string. any \ characters in the individual strings
are escaped as \\.

functions.name: trelby.util.unescapeStrings
documentation: opposite of escapeStrings. takes in a string, returns a list of strings.

functions.name: trelby.util.encodeStr
documentation: return s encoded so that all characters outside the range [32,126] (and
"\\") are escaped.

functions.name: trelby.util.decodeStr
documentation: reverse of encodeStr. if string contains invalid escapes, they're
silently and arbitrarily replaced by something.

functions.name: trelby.util._decodeRepl
documentation: converts "\A4" style matches to their character values.

functions.name: trelby.util.escapeRTF
documentation: return string s escaped for use in RTF.

functions.name: trelby.util.mm2twips
documentation: convert mm to twips (1/1440 inch = 1/20 point).

functions.name: trelby.util.isFixedWidth
documentation: TODO: move all GUI stuff to gutil
return True if given font is a fixed-width one.

functions.name: trelby.util.getTextExtent
documentation: get extent of 's' as (w, h)

functions.name: trelby.util.getFontHeight
documentation: get height of font in pixels

functions.name: trelby.util.getTextHeight
documentation: return how many mm tall given font size is.

functions.name: trelby.util.getTextWidth
documentation: return how many mm wide given text is at given style with given size.

functions.name: trelby.util.createPixelFont
documentation: create a font that's height is at most 'height' pixels. other parameters
are the same as in wx.Font's constructor.

functions.name: trelby.util.setWH
documentation: set widget's client size. if w or h is -1, that dimension is not changed.

functions.name: trelby.util.getSpinValue
documentation: wxMSW doesn't respect the control's min/max values at all, so we have to
implement this ourselves

functions.name: trelby.util.isWordBoundary
documentation: return True if c is not a word character, i.e. is either empty, not an
alphanumeric character or a "'", or is more than one character.

functions.name: trelby.util.isAlnum
documentation: return True if c is an alphanumeric character

functions.name: trelby.util.ensureEndsIn
documentation: make sure s (unicode) ends in suffix (case-insensitively) and return
that. suffix must already be lower-case.

functions.name: trelby.util.splitToWords
documentation: return string 's' split into words (as a list), using isWordBoundary.

functions.name: trelby.util.getWordPrefix
documentation: return two-character prefix of s, using characters a-z only. len(s) must
be at least 2.

functions.name: trelby.util.countInitial
documentation: return count of how many 'ch' characters 's' begins with.

functions.name: trelby.util.multiFind
documentation: searches string 's' for each item of list 'seq', returning True if any
of them were found.

functions.name: trelby.util.sortDict
documentation: put everything from dictionary d into a list as (key, value) tuples,
then sort the list and return that. by default sorts by "desc(value)
asc(key)", but a custom sort function can be given

functions.name: trelby.util.drawLine
documentation: DrawLine-wrapper that makes it easier when the end-point is just
offsetted from the starting point

functions.name: trelby.util.drawText
documentation: draws text aligned somehow. returns a (w, h) tuple of the text extent.

functions.name: trelby.util.finishWindow
documentation: create pad sizer for given window whose controls are in topSizer, with
'pad' pixels of padding on each side, resize window to correct size, and
optionally center it.

functions.name: trelby.util.loadFile
documentation: load at most maxSize (all if -1) bytes from 'filename', returning the
data as a string or None on errors. pops up message boxes with 'frame'
as parent on errors.

functions.name: trelby.util.loadMaybeCompressedFile
documentation: like loadFile, but if file doesn't exist, tries to load a .gz compressed
version of it.

functions.name: trelby.util.writeToFile
documentation: write 'data' to 'filename', popping up a messagebox using 'frame' as
parent on errors. returns True on success.

functions.name: trelby.util.fileExists
documentation: return True if given file exists.

functions.name: trelby.util.findFile
documentation: look for file 'filename' in all the directories listed in 'dirs', which
is a list of absolute directory paths. if found, return the absolute
filename, otherwise None.

functions.name: trelby.util.findFileInPath
documentation: look for file 'filename' in all the directories listed in $PATH. if
found, return the absolute filename, otherwise None.

functions.name: trelby.util._getWindowsPDFViewer
documentation: Get the Windows default PDF viewer path from registry and return that,
or None on errors.

functions.name: trelby.util.getPDFViewer
documentation: Get a tuple of the default PDF viewer path and args for the current platform
and return that, or a tuple of two Nones on errors.

functions.name: trelby.util.getWindowsUnicodeEnvVar
documentation: get a windows environment variable in its native unicode format, or None
if not found

functions.name: trelby.util.showPDF
documentation: show PDF file.

methods.name: trelby.util.FIFO.add
documentation: add item

methods.name: trelby.util.FIFO.get
documentation: get contents as a list, in LIFO order.
an efficient FIFO container of fixed size. can't contain None objects.

classes.name: trelby.util.MyColor
documentation: wx.Colour replacement that can safely be copy.deepcopy'd

classes.name: trelby.util.MyKeyEvent
documentation: fake key event, supports same operations as the real one

methods.name: trelby.util.Key.isValidInputChar

methods.name: trelby.util.Key.toInt
documentation: toInt/fromInt serialize/deserialize to/from a 35-bit integer, laid
out like this:
bits 0-31:  keycode
32:  Control
33:  Alt
34:  Shift

methods.name: trelby.util.Key.fromKE
documentation: construct from wx.KeyEvent
one key press

classes.name: trelby.util.String
documentation: a string-like object that features reasonably fast repeated appends even
for large strings, since it keeps each appended string as an item in a
list.

classes.name: trelby.util.TimerDev
documentation: simple timer class for use during development only

modules.name: trelby.error
documentation: exception classes

classes.name: trelby.watermarkdlg.WatermarkDlg

methods.name: trelby.watermarkdlg.WatermarkDlg.__init__
documentation: sp - screenplay object, from which to generate PDF
prefix - prefix name for the PDF files (unicode)
The watermark tool dialog.

functions.name: trelby.undo.lines2storage
documentation: convert a list of Screenplay.Line objects into an unspecified, but
compact, form of storage. storage2lines will convert this back to the
original form.
the return type is a tuple: (numberOfLines, ...). the number and type of
elements after the first is of no concern to the caller.
implementation notes:
tuple[1]: bool; True if tuple[2] is zlib-compressed
tuple[2]: string; the line objects converted to their string
representation and joined by the "\n" character

functions.name: trelby.undo.storage2lines
documentation: see lines2storage.

functions.name: trelby.undo.memoryUsed
documentation: how much memory is used by the given storage object

functions.name: trelby.undo.mySequenceMatcher
documentation: Our own implementation of difflib.SequenceMatcher, since the actual one
is too slow for our custom needs.
l1, l2 = lists to diff. List elements must have __ne__ defined.
Return a, b, x, y such that l1[a:b] could be replaced with l2[x:y] to
convert l1 into l2.

classes.name: trelby.undo.Base

methods.name: trelby.undo.Base.setEndPos
documentation: set cursor position after the action

methods.name: trelby.undo.Base.memoryUsed
documentation: rough estimate of how much memory is used by this undo object. can
be overridden by subclasses that need something different.

methods.name: trelby.undo.Base.undo
documentation: default implementation for undo. can be overridden by subclasses
that need something different.

methods.name: trelby.undo.Base.redo
documentation: default implementation for redo. can be overridden by subclasses
that need something different.
abstract base class for storing undo history. concrete subclasses
implement undo/redo for specific actions taken on a screenplay.

classes.name: trelby.undo.FullCopy

methods.name: trelby.undo.FullCopy.setAfter
documentation: called after editing action is over to snapshot the "after" state
stores a full copy of the screenplay before/after the action. used by
actions that modify the screenplay globally.
we store the line data as compressed text, not as a list of Line
objects, because it takes much less memory to do so. figures from a
32-bit machine (a 64-bit machine wastes even more space storing Line
objects) from speedTest for a 120-page screenplay (Casablanca):
-Line objects:         1,737 KB, 0.113s
-text, not compressed:   267 KB, 0.076s
-text, zlib fastest(1):  127 KB, 0.090s
-text, zlib medium(6):   109 KB, 0.115s
-text, zlib best(9):     107 KB, 0.126s
-text, bz2 best(9):       88 KB, 0.147s

classes.name: trelby.undo.SinglePara

methods.name: trelby.undo.SinglePara.__init__
documentation: line is any line belonging to the modified paragraph. there is no
requirement for the cursor to be in this paragraph.
stores a single modified paragraph

classes.name: trelby.undo.ManyElems

methods.name: trelby.undo.ManyElems.__init__
documentation: line is any line belonging to the first modified element. there is
no requirement for the cursor to be in this paragraph.
nrOfElemsStart is how many elements there are before the edit
operaton and nrOfElemsEnd is how many there are after. so an edit
operation splitting an element would pass in (1, 2) while an edit
operation combining two elements would pass in (2, 1).
stores N modified consecutive elements

classes.name: trelby.undo.AnyDifference
documentation: stores a single block of changed lines by diffing before/after states of
a screenplay

to output (util.String). pe = PDFExporter.
An abstract base class for all PDF drawing operations.

documentation: used for keeping track of used fonts

methods.name: trelby.pdf.PDFExporter.genStream
documentation: generate a stream object's contents. 's' is all data between

methods.name: trelby.pdf.PDFExporter.getFontForFlags
documentation: get font name to use for given flags. also registers the font in reportlabs if it does not yet exist.

methods.name: trelby.pdf.PDFExporter.escapeStr

methods.name: trelby.pdf.PDFExporter.mm2points
documentation: convert mm to points (1/72 inch).

documentation: convert y coordinate

functions.name: trelby.opts.init
documentation: TODO: Python, at least up to 2.4, does not support Unicode command line
arguments on Windows. Since UNIXes use UTF-8, just assume all command
line arguments are UTF-8 for now, and silently ignore any coding errors
that may result on Windows in some cases.

classes.name: trelby.cfgdlg.MyListBook

methods.name: trelby.cfgdlg.MyListBook.GetPages
documentation: get a list of all the pages

methods.name: trelby.cfgdlg.MyListBook.GetContainingSize
documentation: get (w,h) tuple that's big enough to cover all contained pages

methods.name: trelby.cfgdlg.MyListBook.SetPageSizes
documentation: set all page sizes
WX2.6-FIXME: we can delete this when/if we switch to using wxListBook in
wxWidgets 2.6

methods.name: trelby.cfgdlg.CfgDlg.checkForErrors
documentation: check for errors in each panel

methods.name: trelby.cfgdlg.PDFFontsPanel.checkForErrors
documentation: check that all embedded TrueType fonts are OK

methods.name: trelby.cfgdlg.PDFFontsPanel.getFontPostscriptName
documentation: read TrueType font from given file and return its Postscript name,
or "" on errors.

functions.name: trelby.misc.fromGUI
documentation: convert s, which is returned from the wxWidgets GUI and is an Unicode
string, to a normal string.

functions.name: trelby.misc.toPath
documentation: convert s, which is an Unicode string, to an object suitable for passing
to Python's file APIs. this is either the Unicode string itself, if the
platform supports Unicode-based APIs (and Python has implemented support
for it), or the Unicode string converted to UTF-8 on other platforms.

functions.name: trelby.misc.getBitmap
documentation: return bitmap created from the given file. argument is as for
getFullPath.

functions.name: trelby.misc.getFullPath
documentation: return the absolute path of a file under the install dir. so passing in
"resources/blaa.png" might return "/opt/trelby/resources/blaa.png" for
example.

functions.name: trelby.misc.showText
documentation: helper function for using TextDlg

classes.name: trelby.misc.MyColorSample
documentation: TODO: move all GUI stuff to gutil

classes.name: trelby.misc.MyFSButton
documentation: Custom "exit fullscreen" button for our tab bar. Used so that we have
full control over the button's size.

classes.name: trelby.misc.MyStatus
documentation: custom status control

classes.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.getTabParent
documentation: get the ctrl that the tabbed windows should use as a parent

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.getPageCount
documentation: get page count

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.getSelectedPageIndex
documentation: get selected page index

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.getPage
documentation: get given page

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.add2
documentation: MyTabCtrl2 uses this to register itself with us

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.addPage
documentation: add page

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.setPageSizes
documentation: set all page's sizes

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.selectPage
documentation: select given page

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.deletePage
documentation: delete given page

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.scroll
documentation: try to change the first visible tag by the given amount.

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.calcMaxVisibleTabs
documentation: calculate the maximum number of tabs that we could show with our
current size.

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.getLastVisibleTab
documentation: get last visible tab

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.makeSelectedTabVisible
documentation: make sure selected tab is visible

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.setTabText
documentation: set text for tab 'i' to 's'

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl.setPageChangedFunc
documentation: set function to call when page changes. the function gets a single
integer argument, the index of the new page.
our own version of a tab control, which exists for two reasons: it does
not care where it is physically located, which allows us to combine it
with other controls on a horizontal row, and it consumes less vertical
space than wx.Notebook. note that this control is divided into two parts,
MyTabCtrl and MyTabCtrl2, and both must be created.

classes.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl2

methods.name: trelby.misc.MyTabCtrl2.OnPaint
documentation: we have an OnPaint handler that does nothing in a feeble attempt in
trying to make sure that in the cases when this does get called, as
little (useless) work as possible is done.
second part of MyTabCtrl

classes.name: trelby.misc.ScriptChooserDlg
documentation: dialog that shows two lists of script names, allowing user to choose one
from both. stores indexes of selections in members named 'sel1' and
'sel2' when OK is pressed. 'items' must have at least two items.

classes.name: trelby.misc.CheckBoxItem

methods.name: trelby.misc.CheckBoxItem.getClientData
documentation: return dict which has keys for all selected items' client data.
takes a list of CheckBoxItem's as its parameter. note: this is a
static function.
CheckBoxDlg below handles lists of these

classes.name: trelby.misc.CheckBoxDlg
documentation: shows one or two (one if cbil2 = None) checklistbox widgets with
contents from cbil1 and possibly cbil2, which are lists of
CheckBoxItems. btns[12] are bools for whether or not to include helper
buttons. if OK is pressed, the incoming lists' items' selection status
will be modified.

classes.name: trelby.misc.TextDlg
documentation: shows a multi-line string to the user in a scrollable text control.

classes.name: trelby.misc.TextInputDlg
documentation: ask user for a single-line text input.

classes.name: trelby.misc.KeyDlg
documentation: asks the user for a keypress and stores it.

documentation: used by KeyDlg

classes.name: trelby.misc.MRUFiles

methods.name: trelby.misc.MRUFiles.useMenu
documentation: use given menu. this must be called before any "add" calls.

methods.name: trelby.misc.MRUFiles.getIds
documentation: return (firstMenuId, lastMenuId).

methods.name: trelby.misc.MRUFiles.add
documentation: add item.

methods.name: trelby.misc.MRUFiles.getCount
documentation: return number of items.

methods.name: trelby.misc.MRUFiles.get
documentation: get item number 'i'.
handles the "Most recently used" list of files in a menu.

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.DialogueChart.drawLegend
documentation: draw a single legend for page content bars

classes.name: trelby.dialoguechart.PageInfo

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.PageInfo.addLine
documentation: add one line of given type.

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.PageInfo.getTotalLineCount
documentation: get total number of lines.

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.PageInfo.getLineCount
documentation: get number of lines of given type.

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.PageInfo.addLineToSpeaker
documentation: add one dialogue line for given speaker.

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.PageInfo.getSpeakerLineCount
documentation: get number of lines of dialogue for given character.
keeps track of information for one page

classes.name: trelby.dialoguechart.CharInfo

methods.name: trelby.dialoguechart.CharInfo.addLine
documentation: add a line at given page.
keeps track of each character's dialogue lines.

classes.name: trelby.autocompletion.AutoCompletion

methods.name: trelby.autocompletion.AutoCompletion.load
documentation: load config from string 's'. does not throw any exceptions, silently
ignores any errors, and always leaves config in an ok state.

methods.name: trelby.autocompletion.AutoCompletion.save
documentation: save config into a string and return that.

methods.name: trelby.autocompletion.AutoCompletion.refresh
documentation: fix up invalid values and uppercase everything.

methods.name: trelby.autocompletion.AutoCompletion.getType
documentation: get type's Type, or None if it doesn't exist.
manages auto completion information for a single script.

classes.name: trelby.autocompletion.Type
documentation: auto completion info for one element type

classes.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.makeDicts
documentation: make various dictionaries pointing to the config variables.

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.getDict
documentation: return dictionary containing given type of variable objects, or all
if typeObj is None.

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.getDefault
documentation: get default value of a setting

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.getMin
documentation: get minimum value of a numeric setting

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.getMax
documentation: get maximum value of a numeric setting

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.getMinMax
documentation: get minimum and maximum value of a numeric setting as a (min,max)
tuple.

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.Vars.makeVals
documentation: transform string 's' (loaded from file) into a form suitable for
load() to take.
keep track about one object's variables

methods.name: trelby.mypickle.ConfVar.__init__
documentation: name2 is the name to use while saving/loading the variable. if it's
empty, the variable is not loaded/saved, i.e. is used only
internally.

classes.name: trelby.mypickle.StrLatin1Var
documentation: ISO-8859-1 (Latin 1) string.

classes.name: trelby.mypickle.StrUnicodeVar
documentation: Unicode string.

classes.name: trelby.mypickle.StrBinaryVar
documentation: binary string, can contain anything. characters outside of printable
ASCII (and \ itself) are encoded as \XX, where XX is the hex code of the
character.

classes.name: trelby.mypickle.ElementNameVar
documentation: screenplay.ACTION <-> "Action"

modules.name: trelby.screenplay
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.needsExtraParenIndent
documentation: return True if the line is a parenthetical and not the first line of
that element (such lines need an extra space of indenting).

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.__deepcopy__
documentation: we implement our own custom deepcopy because it's 8-10x faster than
the generic one (times reported by cmdSpeedTest using a 119-page
screenplay):
│                             │ Generic │ Custom │
│ Intel Core Duo T2050 1.6GHz │  0.173s │ 0.020s │

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.save
documentation: save script to a utf-8 encoded string and return that

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.load
documentation: load script from string s and return a (Screenplay, msg) tuple,
where msgs is string (possibly empty) of warnings about the loading
process. fatal errors are indicated by raising a MiscError. note
that this is a static function.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getConfigPart
documentation: lines is an array of strings. if lines[startIndex] == "Begin-$name
", this searches for a string of "End-$name ", takes all the strings
between those two, joins the lines into a single string (lines
separated by a "\n") and returns (string,
line-index-after-the-end-line). returns ("", startIndex) if
startIndex does not contain the start line or startIndex is too big
for 'lines'. raises error.MiscError on errors.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.parseConfigLine
documentation: parse a line containing a config-value in the format detailed in
fileformat.txt. line must have newline stripped from the end
already. returns a (key, value) tuple. if line doesn't match the
format, (None, None) is returned.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.applyCfg
documentation: apply new config.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.saveCfg
documentation: return script config as a string.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generateText
documentation: generate formatted text and return it as a string. if 'dopages' is
True, marks pagination in the output.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generateHtml
documentation: generate HTML output and return it as a string, optionally including
notes.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getElementsAsList
documentation: Return screenplay as list of tuples of the form (elType, elText).
forced linebreaks are represented as \n characters.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generateFDX
documentation: Generate a Final Draft XML file and return as string.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generateFountain
documentation: generate Fountain and return it as a string.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generateRTF
documentation: generate RTF and return it as a string.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generatePDF
documentation: generate PDF and return it as a string. assumes paginate/reformat is
100% correct for the screenplay. isExport is True if this is an
"export to file" operation, False if we're just going to launch a
PDF viewer with the data.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generatePML
documentation: Same arguments as generatePDF, but returns a PML document.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.generatePMLPage
documentation: generate one page of PML data and return it.
if forPDF is True, output is meant for PDF generation.
if doExtra is False, omits headers and other stuff that is
automatically added, i.e. outputs only actual screenplay lines. also
text style/capitalization is not done 100% correctly. this should
only be True for callers that do not show the results in any way,
just calculate things based on text positions.
can also return None, which means pagination is not up-to-date and
the given page number doesn't point to a valid page anymore, and the
caller should stop calling this since all pages have been generated
(assuming 1-to-n calling sequence).

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getTopLine
documentation: get topLine, clamping it to the valid range in the process.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.setTopLine
documentation: set topLine, clamping it to the valid range.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.reformatRange
documentation: reformat part of the screenplay. par1 is line number of paragraph to
start at, par2 the same for the ending one, inclusive.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.wrapLine
documentation: wraps a single line into however many lines are needed, according to
the type's width. doesn't modify the input line, returns a list of
new lines.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.rewrapPara
documentation: rewrap paragraph starting at given line. returns the number of lines
in the wrapped paragraph. if line1 is -1, rewraps paragraph
containing self.line. maintains cursor position correctness.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.rewrapPrevPara
documentation: rewraps paragraph previous to current one.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.rewrapElem
documentation: rewrap element starting at given line. if line is -1, rewraps
element containing self.line.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getParaFirstIndexFromLine
documentation: get first index of paragraph

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getParaLastIndexFromLine
documentation: get last index of paragraph

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getSceneNumber
documentation: return scene number for the given line. if line is -1, return 0.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.elemsDistance
documentation: return how many elements one must advance to get from element
containing line1 to element containing line2. line1 must be <=
line2, and either line can be anywhere in their respective elements.
returns 0 if they're in the same element, 1 if they're in
consecutive elements, etc.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.needsMore
documentation: returns true if 'line', which must be the last line on a page, needs
(MORE) after it and the next page needs a "SOMEBODY (cont'd)".

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getPrevSpeaker
documentation: starting at line, go backwards until a line with type of CHARACTER
and lb of LAST is found, and return that line's text, possibly
upper-cased if CHARACTER's config for export says so.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getCharCount
documentation: return total number of characters in script

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.convertTypeTo
documentation: convert element(s) to given type
- if multiple elements are selected, all are changed
- if not, the change is applied to element under cursor.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.joinLines
documentation: join lines 'line' and 'line + 1' and position cursor at the join
position.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.splitLine
documentation: split current line at current column position.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.splitElement
documentation: split element at current position. newType is type to give to the
new element.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.deleteChar
documentation: delete character at given position and optionally position
cursor there.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.setLineTypes
documentation: set line types from 'line' to the end of the element to 'lt'.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.page2lines
documentation: return (startLine, endLine) for given page number (1-based). if
pageNr is out of bounds, it is clamped to the valid range. if
pagination is out of date and the lines no longer exist, they are
clamped to the valid range as well.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getPageNumbers
documentation: return a list of all page numbers as strings.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getSceneLocations
documentation: return a list of all scene locations in a [(sceneNumber, startLine),
...] format. if script does not start with a scene line, that scene
is not included in this list. note that the sceneNumber in the
returned list is a string, not a number.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getSceneNames
documentation: return a dictionary of all scene names (single-line text elements
only, upper-cased, values = None).

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getCharacterNames
documentation: return a dictionary of all character names (single-line text
elements only, lower-cased, values = None).

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getWord
documentation: get next word, starting at (line, col). line must be valid, but col
can point after the line's length, in which case the search starts
at (line + 1, 0). returns (word, line, col), where word is None if
at end of script, and (line, col) point to the start of the word.
note that this only handles words that are on a single line.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.isAtEndOfParen
documentation: returns True if we're at second-to-last character of PAREN element,
and last character is ")"

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.tabMakesNew
documentation: returns True if pressing TAB at current position would make a new
element, False if it would just change element's type.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.clearAutoComp
documentation: if auto-completion is active, clear it and return True. otherwise
return False.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.pageScrollAutoComp
documentation: page up (dir == -1) or page down (dir == 1) was pressed and we're in
auto-comp mode, handle it.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getMatchingText
documentation: get a list of strings (single-line text elements for now) that start
with 'text' (not case sensitive) and are of of type 'type'. also
mixes in the type's default items from config. ignores current line.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getMarkedLines
documentation: returns pair (start, end) of marked lines, inclusive. if mark is
after the end of the script (text has been deleted since setting
it), returns a valid pair (by truncating selection to current
end). returns None if no lines marked.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getMarkedColumns
documentation: returns pair (start, end) (inclusive) of marked columns for the
given line (line must be inside the marked lines). 'marked' is the
value returned from getMarkedLines. if marked column is invalid
(text has been deleted since setting the mark), returns a valid pair
by truncating selection as needed. returns None on errors.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.isLineMarked
documentation: checks if a line is marked. 'marked' is the value returned from
getMarkedLines.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.getSelectedAsCD
documentation: get selected text as a ClipData object, optionally deleting it from
the script. if nothing is selected, returns None.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.paste
documentation: paste data into script. clines is a list of Line objects.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.capitalizeNeeded
documentation: returns true if a character, inserted at current position, would
need to be capitalized as a start of a sentence.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.findError
documentation: find next error in screenplay, starting at given line. returns
(line, msg) tuple, where line is -1 if no error was found and the
line number otherwise where the error is, and msg is a description
of the error

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.compareScripts
documentation: compare this script to sp2 (Screenplay), return a PDF file (as a
string) of the differences, or None if the scripts are identical.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.gotoPos
documentation: move to line,col, and if mark is True, set mark there

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.removeElementTypes
documentation: remove all lines whose element types are in tdict as keys.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.setMark
documentation: set mark at given position

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.clearMark
documentation: clear mark

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.maybeMark
documentation: if doIt is True and mark is not yet set, set it at current position.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.validatePos
documentation: make sure current line and column are within the valid bounds.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.cmdPost
documentation: this must be called after each command (all functions named fooCmd
are commands)

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.cmd
documentation: helper function for calling commands. name is the name of the
command, e.g. "moveLeft".

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.cmdChars
documentation: call addCharCmd for each character in s. ONLY MEANT TO BE USED IN
TEST CODE.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.abortCmd
documentation: aborts stuff, like selection, auto-completion, etc

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.selectSceneCmd
documentation: select all text of current scene

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.selectAllCmd
documentation: select all text of the screenplay. sets mark at beginning and moves
cursor to the end.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.tabCmd
documentation: either creates a new element or converts the current one to
nextTypeTab, depending on circumstances.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.toPrevTypeTabCmd
documentation: switch current element to prevTypeTab.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.addCharCmd
documentation: add character cs.char if it's a valid one.

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.canUndo
documentation: return True if we can undo

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay.canRedo
documentation: return True if we can redo

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Screenplay._validate
documentation: check script for internal consistency. raises an AssertionError on
errors. ONLY MEANT TO BE USED IN TEST CODE.
screenplay

classes.name: trelby.screenplay.Line

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.Line.fromStr
documentation: opposite of __str__. NOTE: only meant for storing data internally by
the program! NOT USABLE WITH EXTERNAL INPUT DUE TO COMPLETE LACK OF
ERROR CHECKING!
one line in a screenplay

classes.name: trelby.screenplay.Mark
documentation: used to keep track of selected area. this marks one of the end-points,
while the other one is the current position.

classes.name: trelby.screenplay.ClipData
documentation: data held in internal clipboard.

classes.name: trelby.screenplay.CommandState
documentation: stuff we need when handling commands in Screenplay.

classes.name: trelby.screenplay.PageList

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.PageList.addPage
documentation: add page to page list if it's not already there

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.PageList.__iadd__
documentation: merge two PageLists

methods.name: trelby.screenplay.PageList.__str__
documentation: return textual representation of pages where consecutive pages are
formatted as "x-y". example: "3, 5-8, 11".
keeps a collection of page numbers from a given screenplay, and allows
formatting of the list intelligently, e.g. "4-7, 9, 11-16".

functions.name: trelby.namesdlg.readNames
documentation: if not already loaded, read the name database from disk and store it.
returns False on errors.

methods.name: trelby.namesdlg.MyListCtrl.OnGetItemImage
documentation: for some reason this must be overridden as well, otherwise we get
assert failures under windows.

functions.name: trelby.gutil.listBoxSelect
documentation: this contains misc GUI-related functions
since at least GTK 1.2's single-selection listbox is buggy in that if we
don't deselect the old item manually, it does multiple selections, we
have this function that does the following:
1) deselects current selection, if any
2) select the item with the given index

functions.name: trelby.gutil.listBoxAdd
documentation: add (name, cdata) to the listbox at the correct place, determined by
cmp(cdata1, cdata2).

functions.name: trelby.gutil.createStockButton
documentation: create stock button.

functions.name: trelby.gutil.btnDblClick
documentation: wxWidgets has a bug in 2.6 on wxGTK2 where double clicking on a button
does not send two wx.EVT_BUTTON events, only one. since the wxWidgets
maintainers do not seem interested in fixing this
(http://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?func=detail&aid=1449838&group_id=9863&atid=109863),
we work around it ourselves by binding the left mouse button double
click event to the same callback function on the buggy platforms.

functions.name: trelby.gutil.showTempPDF
documentation: show PDF document 'pdfData' in an external viewer program. writes out a
temporary file, first deleting all old temporary files, then opens PDF
viewer application. 'mainFrame' is used as a parent for message boxes in
case there are any errors.

methods.name: trelby.headersdlg.HeadersDlg.updateGui
documentation: update listbox

methods.name: trelby.headersdlg.HeadersDlg.updateHeaderGui
documentation: update selected header stuff

functions.name: trelby.myimport.importAstx
documentation: like importTextFile, but for Adobe Story files.

functions.name: trelby.myimport.importFadein
documentation: like importTextFile, but for fadein files.

functions.name: trelby.myimport.importCeltx
documentation: like importTextFile, but for Celtx files.

functions.name: trelby.myimport.importFDX
documentation: like importTextFile, but for Final Draft files.

functions.name: trelby.myimport.importFountain
documentation: import Fountain files.
http://fountain.io

functions.name: trelby.myimport.importTextFile
documentation: import text file from fileName, return list of Line objects for the
screenplay or None if something went wrong. returned list always
contains at least one line.

functions.name: trelby.myimport.setType
documentation: go through indents, find the one with maximum value in something, and
set its linetype to given lt.

functions.name: trelby.myimport.findIndent
documentation: go through indents calling func(it, *vars) on each. return indent count
for the indent func returns True, or -1 if it returns False for each.

classes.name: trelby.myimport.Indent
documentation: information about one indent level in imported text files.

classes.name: trelby.headers.Headers

methods.name: trelby.headers.Headers.addDefaults
documentation: create standard headers

methods.name: trelby.headers.Headers.getNrOfLines
documentation: return how many header lines there are. includes number of empty
lines after possible headers.

methods.name: trelby.headers.Headers.generatePML
documentation: add headers to given page. 'pageNr' must be a string.
a script's headers.

classes.name: trelby.headers.HeaderString

methods.name: trelby.headers.HeaderString.load
documentation: parse information from s, which must be a string created by __str__,
and set object state accordingly. keeps default settings on any
errors, does not throw any exceptions.
sample of the format: '1,0,r,,${PAGE}.'
a single header string

classes.name: trelby.locations.Locations

methods.name: trelby.locations.Locations.load
documentation: load from string 's'. does not throw any exceptions and silently
ignores any errors.

methods.name: trelby.locations.Locations.save
documentation: save to a string and return that.

methods.name: trelby.locations.Locations.refresh
documentation: refresh location list against the given scene names (in the format
returned by Screenplay.getSceneNames()). removes unknown and
duplicate scenes from locations, and if that results in a location
with 0 scenes, removes that location completely. also upper-cases
all the scene names, sorts the lists, first each location list's
scenes, and then the locations based on the first scene of the
location.
manages location-information for a single screenplay. a "location" is a
single place that can be referred to using multiple scene names, e.g.
INT. MOTEL ROOM - DAY
INT. MOTEL ROOM - DAY - 2 HOURS LATER
INT. MOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

functions.name: trelby.fontinfo.getMetrics
documentation: character widths and general font information for each font. acquired
from the PDF font metrics. ((width / 1000) * point_size) / 72.0 = how
many inches wide that character is.
all Courier-* fonts have characters 600 units wide.
get the FontMetrics object for the given style

methods.name: trelby.fontinfo.FontMetrics.getTextWidth
documentation: calculate width of 'text' in 'size', and return it in 1/72 inch
units.

modules.name: trelby.config
documentation: see fileformat.txt for more detailed information about the various
defines found here.

functions.name: trelby.config.getTIs
documentation: get TypeInfos

classes.name: trelby.config.TypeInfo
documentation: non-changing information about an element type

classes.name: trelby.config.TextType
documentation: text type

classes.name: trelby.config.Type
documentation: script-specific information about an element type

classes.name: trelby.config.TypeGlobal
documentation: global information about an element type

classes.name: trelby.config.Command
documentation: command (an action in the main program)

classes.name: trelby.config.FontInfo
documentation: information about one screen font

classes.name: trelby.config.PDFFontInfo

methods.name: trelby.config.PDFFontInfo.refresh
documentation: fix up invalid values.
information about one PDF font

classes.name: trelby.config.Config

methods.name: trelby.config.Config.load
documentation: load config from string 's'. does not throw any exceptions, silently
ignores any errors, and always leaves config in an ok state.

methods.name: trelby.config.Config.save
documentation: save config into a string and return that.

methods.name: trelby.config.Config.recalc
documentation: fix up all invalid config values and recalculate all variables
dependent on other variables.
if doAll is False, enforces restrictions only on a per-variable
basis, e.g. doesn't modify variable v2 based on v1's value. this is
useful when user is interactively modifying v1, and it temporarily
strays out of bounds (e.g. when deleting the old text in an entry
box, thus getting the minimum value), which would then possibly
modify the value of other variables which is not what we want.

methods.name: trelby.config.Config.getPDFFont
documentation: get a PDFFontInfo object for the given font type (PDF_FONT_*)

methods.name: trelby.config.Config.getPDFFontIds
documentation: return a tuple of all the PDF font types
per-script config, each script has its own one of these.

classes.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.load
documentation: load config from string 's'. does not throw any exceptions, silently
ignores any errors, and always leaves config in an ok state.

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.save
documentation: save config into a string and return that.

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.recalc
documentation: fix up all invalid config values.

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.addShiftKeys
documentation: add SHIFT+Key alias for all keys bound to movement commands, so
selection-movement works.

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.removeKey
documentation: remove key (int) from given cmd

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.getConflictingKeys
documentation: get textual description of conflicting keys, or None if no
conflicts.

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.setDefaults
documentation: set default values that vary depending on platform, wxWidgets
version, etc. this is not at the end of __init__ because
non-interactive uses have no needs for these.

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.setDefaultFonts
documentation: set default fonts

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGlobal.findPDFViewer
documentation: set PDF viewer program to the best one found on the machine.
global config. there is only ever one of these active.

classes.name: trelby.config.ConfigGui

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGui.tt2fi
documentation: TextType -> FontInfo

methods.name: trelby.config.ConfigGui.lt2textColor
documentation: line type -> wx.Colour
config stuff that are wxwindows objects, so can't be in normal
ConfigGlobal (deepcopy dies)

classes.name: trelby.trelby.GlobalData

methods.name: trelby.trelby.GlobalData.setViewMode
documentation: set viewmode, the parameter is one of the VIEWMODE_ defines.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.GlobalData.load
documentation: load from string 's'. does not throw any exceptions and silently
ignores any errors.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.GlobalData.save
documentation: save to a string and return that.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.GlobalData.saveScDict
documentation: save global spell checker dictionary to disk
keeps (some) global data

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyPanel.OnScrollbarFocus
documentation: we never want the scrollbar to get the keyboard focus, pass it on to
the main widget

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.refreshCache
documentation: update stuff that depends on configuration / view mode etc.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.saveFile
documentation: save script to given filename. returns True on success.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.getExportText
documentation: generate exportable text from given screenplay, or None.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.isLineVisible
documentation: texts = gd.vm.getScreen(self, False)[0], or None, in which case it's
called in this function.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.isUntouched
documentation: returns true if there are no contents at all and we're not
attached to any file

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.updateCommon
documentation: update GUI elements shared by all scripts, like statusbar etc

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.applyCfg
documentation: apply per-script config

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.applyGlobalCfg
documentation: apply global config

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.getExportable
documentation: return an exportable, paginated Screenplay object, or None if for
some reason that's not possible / wanted. 'action' is the name of
the action, e.g. "export" or "print", that'll be done to the script,
and is used in dialogue with the user if needed.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.pageCmd
documentation: page up (dir == -1) or page down (dir == 1) was pressed, handle it.
cs = CommandState.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyCtrl.OnCut
documentation: returns True if something was deleted

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyFrame.findPage
documentation: iterates over all tabs and finds out the corresponding page number
for the given panel.

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyFrame.getCtrls
documentation: get list of MyCtrl objects for all open scripts

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyFrame.isModifications
documentation: returns True if any open script has been modified

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyFrame.openScript
documentation: open script, in the current tab if it's untouched, or in a new one
otherwise

methods.name: trelby.trelby.MyFrame.OnFocus
documentation: If we get focus, pass it on to ctrl.
